channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Name That Toon Contest
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CISA: Hackers now exploit SolarWinds Serv-U flaw to crash servers
item.description: CISA warned today that hackers are now actively exploiting a recently patched high-severity SolarWinds Serv-U flaw to crash servers. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
item.description: Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Chinese APT deploys new malware to keep access to hacked networks
item.description: A Chinese espionage group tracked as UNC5221 has been accessing Microsoft 365 environments using the Brickstorm backdoor and previously undocumented malware named Plenet and AgentPSD. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: IronWorm and New Miasma Worm Variant Hit npm in Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Multiple software supply chain attacks have hit the npm ecosystem, with threat actors using both malicious and poisoned versions of over 50 legitimate packages to distribute a Rust-based information s...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Dark web Nemesis Market vendor gets 26 years for selling drugs
item.description: A California man was sentenced to more than 26 years in federal prison for trafficking fentanyl and methamphetamine through Nemesis Market, one of the world's largest dark web marketplaces. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Android Spyware Asin Targets Arabic Users via Fake News, PDF and War Map Apps
item.description:
Arabic-speaking users have emerged as the target of a new Android spyware codenamed Asin, according to findings from ESET.
The Slovakian cybersecurity company said it first detected the malware sprea...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Over 900 US gas station tank gauge systems exposed to attacks
item.description: Over 900 automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems across the United States, used to monitor fuel and chemical storage tanks across various critical infrastructure sectors, have been found exposed online and...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Adaptive, Agentic AI Worms Loom as Next Enterprise Threat
item.description: AI worms, or "viruses with wings and brains," adapt to new environments, seek out vulnerabilities, and will likely strike within a year, researchers say....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: What 2026 DBIR Confirms: Attacks Are Living in the Browser
item.description: Phishing, shadow AI, malicious extensions, and credential theft increasingly happen inside the browser. Keep Aware explains what the 2026 Verizon DBIR reveals about browser-layer security gaps and mod...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Trump AI Order Seeks Voluntary Frontier Model Testing
item.description: The White House's executive order establishes voluntary framework for early government access to frontier models while investing in federal security....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Threat Cluster OP-512 Targets Microsoft IIS Servers with Custom Web Shell Framework
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously unreported threat cluster dubbed OP-512 (where "OP" stands for "opponent") that has been observed targeting Microsoft Internet Information Servic...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Only 10% of SOCs Say They’re Getting Excellent Value From AI. Here’s What the Second Wave Has to Deliver
item.description: Eighteen months ago, the AI SOC was a marketing line. Today it's a budget item. The category has crossed over from interesting to inevitable, with billions of dollars now flowing into AI-powered secur...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Everest Forms Pro WordPress Plugin Flaw to Take Over Sites
item.description: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical security flaw in Everest Forms Pro, a WordPress plugin with about 4,000 active installations, to execute arbitrary code, leading to a complete site com...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Scams Are Already Live: Fake Sites, Banking Malware, and Stolen Logins
item.description:
Security researchers and the FBI are warning that a wave of FIFA-themed fraud is already hitting World Cup 2026 fans, days before the June 11 kickoff.
Recent reports describe thousands of lookalike F...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco warns of unpatched SD-WAN zero-day exploited in attacks
item.description: On Thursday, Cisco warned of a high-severity, unpatched zero-day in the Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (tracked as CVE-2026-20245) actively exploited in attacks enabling root privilege escalation. [......
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: PCPJack Hijacks 230 AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure Servers for Covert SMTP Relay Network
item.description:
The threat actor known as PCPJack has hijacked cloud servers associated with Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure to create a covert SMTP email relay network.
"Compromised bus...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Rust-Written IronWorm Hits NPM Supply Chain
item.description: Like Shai-Hulud, the campaign targets developers to steal credentials and reuses them to propagate across the software supply channel....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Brave Software releases Origin for a paid, bloat-free browsing experience
item.description: Brave has announced the public release of Brave Origin, a paid minimalist version of its browser that strips out cryptocurrency, AI, rewards, and other monetization-focused features. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hola Browser for Windows compromised to deliver cryptominer
item.description: The Windows version of the Hola Browser has been compromised in a supply chain attack that delivered an undeclared executable identified by researchers as a cryptocurrency miner. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
item.description: One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 4 Critical Threats Where Attackers Have the Advantage
item.description: Gartner analysts issued a call to action to bolster defenses against several emerging critical threats, such as deepfakes and prompt injections....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Credit card theft campaign abuses Stripe to host stolen payment info
item.description: A new Magecart campaign is using Stripe's API infrastructure to host the credit card-stealing payload and the data exfiltrated from checkout pages. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DentaQuest data breach exposed info of 2.6 million accounts
item.description: A data breach at the dental benefits administrator DentaQuest has reportedly exposed the sensitive data of 2.6 million accounts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cisco Patches CVE-2026-20230 in Unified CM as Exploit Code Goes Public
item.description:
Cisco has patched a bug in Unified Communications Manager that lets an unauthenticated attacker on the network write files to the box and, from there, climb to root.
It is tracked as CVE-2026-20230, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: UN food agency discloses breach affecting 600,000 Gaza households
item.description: The United Nations' World Food Programme (WFP), the world's largest humanitarian organization, revealed over the weekend that its self-registration application (SRA) for Palestine was breached. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New IronWorm malware hits 36 packages in npm supply-chain attack
item.description: A new supply-chain attack has infected 36 packages on the Node Package Manager (npm) index with infostealer malware called IronWorm. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Claude Code GitHub Action Flaw Let One Malicious Issue Hijack Repositories
item.description: A security researcher found a flaw in Anthropic's Claude Code GitHub Action that let an attacker take over vulnerable public repositories running it, with nothing more than a single opened GitHub issu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Agentic AI Is Transforming Defense, But Only Secure IT Infrastructure Will Maximize It
item.description: Over the past several weeks, the cybersecurity community has been reminded how quickly frontier and agentic AI in defense networks can challenge our assumptions. When Anthropic's Claude Mythos model w...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
item.description: Organizations are growing serious about what nation’s rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
